reviews.review_msg: he is new. his notes are extremely unclear and messy. i wouldn't recommend him if you didn't ace your econ 100 level courses. he is willing to help though didn't do a good job in explaining the theories.

Avg prof. Tends to go off on tangents & lose sight of the material.Uses lot of jargon which can be confusing for some not well versed in macro issues. U find urself losing focus as he jumps from one topic to the nxt & leaves u with confused mishmash of things u try to make sense of. Avoid if u prefer to have organization and structure to lectures.

His exams are fair if you practice past midterms and finals. Qs are very similar. Read his notes and the blue sections in the textbook and you'll be fine. I did well on MT1, failed MT2, and ended up with an A-, so it's possible to do well even if you bomb one midterm. Also, he's really approachable so ask questions!

Geoff's lectures are very interesting.  The class is worth it just for those.  He doesn't spoon feed the material.  Self-directed studying is a must.  Questions on exams really test if you've acquired the knowledge, rather than just memorized facts.  Nice guy all around.
Of course you have to read the textbook carefully and the lecture notes. He's exam was pretty hard.
very interesting lectures.  helpful and fair guy.
focus on the past exams will be leading you to succeed.......the exam questions at least 90% similiar to the past exam and assignment...

Does a good job of keeping macroeconomics interesting. His somewhat random tangents are nice as they usually include real life examples of topics and it also gives a break from the lecture material which is pretty hard to take it all in during lecture (especially at 8:30am). Overall one of the best ECON profs at SFU, he gets my seal of approval.
very easy course. You don't need to buy the text book because he does not use the text at all! Just read his lecture slides and you will do find on the midterms and final.

i donno abt how he teaches 291 but in 305 i found his lectures rather interesting. he explains things clearly and puts good arguments behind his reasoning (but i rarely came to class b/c it was 8:30am) his assignments are harder than his exams (which is good for learning) but thankfully the TA marked leniently. keep up w/ textbook reading tho.

Good hearted. On the exam he lets you survive. But speaks too fast. You can hardly record it. Knowledge is not thorough. But will not agree that he for instance does not understand Hamiltonians himself. Jumps around between the topics, does not hold steady focus.

not monotone, great voice....don't go to class if you don't want to, just read the book, but make sure you analyze the graph; there are only so many different variables they can adjust for questions, so just understand how things shift.
I don't have much say about Geoff because I did not go to class at all, but TA Sascha was extremly helpful. If you understand IS-LM curve, you will get a good mark.
Spoke very clearly...but very monotoned.  He is extremely knowledgeable in Economics.  Speaks very fast.  Notes are long...but is a must to pass the course.
really easy prof. notes are a mess and rants like there's no tomorrow. something about his voice is hypnotic and makes me zone out almost immediately and/or fall asleep.

Hated this course but got a good grade. Just because of that doesn't necessarily mean Dunbar is a good prof. Notes were jumping all over the place from chapters to another, they are not even in a chronological order. Gives you a lot of notes to copy down and expects you to listen to his nonstop yapping. Overall, poor prof but TAs were easy going.

His exams are reasonably hard, but shouldn't be a problem for those who focus on real-life economic events. Good thing is the course only got 2 MTs and a final, but 2 bad things attached: mts are only given an hr, and his order of teaching is confusing (syllabus is outdated, and ch are not taught in order). Also hes got bad handwriting!

Seems pretty knowledgeable about the subject but I hardly ever listen to him talk in class because I'm always too busy copying down his notes. In the 2nd half of the course he actually handwrites the notes on the overhead slides.bI spend half the time trying to figure out what he wrote... His midterm exams are fair though...

Wish he told us about midterms earlier. But his notes are a good indication of what is to be expected on the midterms. I found them fair and simple and the TA marking them was generous. Enjoyed this class even though there was way to much info in long lecs. Lots of examples related to current events. Was it honestly that hard?....
I regret taking this class with him...it should be an easy class.  btw I'm wondering if some of the good comments here about him are written by himself.

The midterm was fair and had an average of about 72. I barely even opened my textbook and just from his notes I was able to score in the 80s. Learn how to take proper notes people. You don't have to copy everything word for word. His voice is far from monotone and the tangents into real world examples are very helpful in understanding the material.

The midterm was a killer!...he said studying the notes would be enough but there was sth that's not in the notes but in the textbook on the exam...my god why are you doing this...  and he didn't even tell us there was lecture before the midterm...
1.sure he's pasionate for macroeconomics.             2.lots of notes.        3.goes too detailed(out of question) when explaining things. 4. boring lecture, non-stop talking and mono tone.

do not rely on lecture notes...also he explains things very detailed sometimes its not clear. anyhow, interesting subject and he is very passionate about it. read the textbook 4 times and you are set for the final.

Simply put, if you want to experience the boredom of economics, and/or get yourself killed, come to his class. If you don't want to believe what I said, it's ok. However, don't ever complain once you are in his class.

He gives us the wrong course outline and doesn't correct it.  Who the heck knows what he'll be teaching the following lecture?  Unbelievably unorganized! Told us the midterm date could be on week6 but not it's on week8. Thanks Geof, now I have 2 midterms on the same day, and technically 4 midterms on a single week.

This guy gives lecture notes which are not posted online...and they are too much for me to copy. My hands are always sore after every class. He also keeps on talking while we are copying the notes. Who has the time to focus on what he's saying. In addition, he also asks why no one's asking him questions. What can i say? definitely overrated!

I haven't taken the midterms so I'll not comment on his exams. I have no idea why the previous ppl rate him so high, but in my opinion he sucks.  He doesn't reply u email if he doens't feel like to. He doesn't tell u the midterm dates on the first day of class, nor the range for the midterms.
Super good prof. Knows what he's talking about and very approachable. He's got a good sense of humor too!

The course is hard without a doubt. Its hard to do well in a topic you aren't interested in and that's exactly why this guy is such a good prof! He makes you want to know more, he talks about current events and almost always what you're learning in class he applies to the real world! He challenges you to apply what your learning to everyday life!
it's not an easy course..needs a lot of effors..but he's a very good prof! if you like macro, take it with him!
Very interesting prof. Study his notes, don't bother reading the textbook for his exams.
Dunbar is very interested in the subject matter, which makes the lectures pretty good for econ. His exams are fair.
Fantastic, really made the course interesting. Always helpful if you have any questions, and a very fair marker.
Excellent professor, enjoyable class, great personality. Would love to take any course with him again, thank you...
a lot of math in this course, if you are not good in math, then don't take him...
lots of complicated math!!!! take him IFF if u'r strong in calculas......... ok-lecture. hard exams, time-consuming assignments......... really gotta put lots of effort to get good grades. g'luck

He thinks his exams are simple.. and it's obviously not so easy. It was a lot more math than i expected - and they're very complicated one - asks you to find 2, sometimes 3, unknown variables in an equation with very little information given. A real nice and interesting prof, good to go for if you're strong in math.
fair prof, interesting lectures , knows what he is talking about

He is a nice guy ... and you will for sure to get a good grade if you understand his materials from notes. However, the point is he has so much materials relating to calculous and algebra which makes it almost impossible to understand. The math part always worth 40% of his exams.If you are not the calculous kind of person, dont take it.

You couldn't ask for a better prof. Always happy to help & does his best to explain material. Excellent notes summarizing text and does his best to make the class interesting. Make sure you understand the assignments before the midterm & final!

one of the best at SFU..interesting lectures, very passionate about the subject....however, u gotta know ur calculas...as for the final and the midterm ,, very similar to assignments and other problems done in class
The best econ professor I've had at SFU. High math content, but generous marker. Very approachable, helpful and seems to have a genuine interest in teaching and is passionate about economics.
No Comments

The final was soooooo hard! If you are not good at number crunching then do not, I repeat, do not take his class. The math involved is too complex for me. His lectures are not bad but he gives a lots of notes! Prereq should be Econ 331!
Nice prof with good notes. Fair exam
He loves Calculus. If you are good at it, u can take him. Otherwise, avoid him and choose other profs

he is a great prof., very clear, good notes, and very helpful, however you better know your calculas if you take this class with him......exams are very simillar to assignments and his notes are sufficient to get a good grade..

Dunbar is a nice and friendly guy. However, he goes through the material too quickly and uses too much math in his lectures. He needs to give us more example so that we can associate what he is teaching us with reality. Midterm is almost all math! Better know your algebra, statistics and calculus!
Interesting lectures. Good notes. But he's some kind of calculus demon.....expectations and speed of covering the math are way beyond the prerequisite math.
